title: The Warning Note
text: A torn piece of paper, handwritten in urgent script: 'Someone at this gala is not who they say they are. The Sapphire is in danger. I tried to warn Dr. Mehta but she would not listen. If you find this, look for the person who does not belong.' The handwriting is unidentified. The paper is slightly crumpled as though it was held tightly by nervous hands.

title: The Replica Gemstone (possible red herring)
text: A small blue gemstone sitting in a velvet pouch with a tiny handwritten tag attached: 'REPLICA -- display model only. The real Sapphire of Shadows has a faint silver vein running through its center. This one does not.' Whoever had this replica knew the real gem well enough to source a convincing substitute. This required advance research and planning -- not something you arrange on the night.

title: The Fabric Scrap
text: A small piece of dark fabric -- roughly two inches, with frayed, jagged edges -- found on the floor near the display alcove entrance. The fabric appears to be from the lining of a formal garment: a blazer or structured jacket. It is not from Dr. Mehta's clothing. Someone else was in that alcove and something caught on a sharp edge.

title: The Guest List Anomaly
text: A printed copy of the official gala guest list. Most entries have institutional affiliations or named mutual contacts. One entry stands out: 'Sana -- Independent collector. No institutional affiliation. Registered via personal invitation six months ago.' Six months ago was shortly after the Sapphire's discovery was first reported in a private collector's newsletter. No other guest on the list registered that early or under the 'independent collector' category.

title: The Blurry Security Photo (possible red herring)
text: A blurry security camera screenshot printed on paper. A figure is visible moving near the display alcove at 8:40 PM -- seven minutes before the theft alarm was raised. The face is not clear but the figure is wearing a dark formal outfit and carrying something held close to their body. Timestamp printed at the bottom: 20:40:17.

title: The Disabled Display Case
text: A handwritten forensics note found near the display alcove: 'The locking mechanism on the Sapphire display case was not forced open -- it was bypassed electronically. This requires either a manufacturer override code or a custom bypass device. This is not something improvised on the night. Whoever did this arrived with the device already prepared, meaning they researched and sourced it weeks in advance.' This eliminates opportunistic theft completely. This was a professional, premeditated operation.

title: The Encrypted USB Drive
text: A USB drive found taped under a surface near the display area. A small paper label in neat handwriting reads: 'Sapphire scan data -- dimensions, weight, spectral signature, unique identifiers. File last accessed 3 weeks before the gala.' Whoever accessed this file knew exactly what the Sapphire looked like, how much it weighed, and how to identify it before ever seeing it in person. This was not curiosity. This was research for a planned theft.

title: The Collector Contact Card
text: A small printed card found tucked inside a book near the display area. Front side: 'V. Orlov -- Private Acquisitions. Specializing in rare stones and artifacts. Discreet. International.' Back side, written in a different ink as if by a second person: 'Confirm when item is secured. Transfer on delivery.' The deal was arranged before tonight. Someone at this gala already has a buyer waiting and a transaction ready to execute.

title: The Hidden Compartment Bag
text: A small designer clutch bag found tucked behind items near the storage room area. It looks completely ordinary from the outside. Pressing the bottom panel reveals a hidden compartment -- currently empty, but lined with soft dark velvet cut to exactly the dimensions of the Sapphire. Inside the main compartment: a lip gloss, a receipt for a gemstone transport case purchased two weeks ago, and a boarding pass stub to Vienna dated for tomorrow morning.

title: The Clear Security Photo
text: A second security camera image -- this one sharp and clear. Timestamp: 20:41:03. The same figure from the blurry photo has turned slightly. Their face is now partially visible. The image is labeled at the bottom: 'Enhanced still from gala security feed. Subject identified near display alcove at 20:41. Subject carrying item consistent in size with the Sapphire case.' Anyone who sees this image and knows the people at this gala will be able to identify the figure.
